name: Open X-Embodiment
scale: 15 种机器人，100W+ 轨迹
notes: 跨具身最大开源数据集，RT-X 训练必备。覆盖多种机器人形态，适合泛化学习研究。
description:
**Open X-Embodiment** is a cross-embodiment robot learning dataset jointly released by **Google DeepMind** and **34 research institutions** worldwide.

It aggregates 60+ independent datasets from **22 different robot morphologies**, totaling over **1 million real robot manipulation trajectories**. All data is unified under the **RLDS (Reinforcement Learning Datasets Standard)** format, covering vision, proprioception, and action modalities.

This dataset is the core training source for **RT-1, RT-2, RT-X** and other robot foundation models, and remains the largest and most influential open-source robot learning dataset to date.

dataFormat.schema: episode → step → observation / action / reward / discount
dataFormat.layout: RLDS 格式（tf.data.Dataset of Episodes）；每 episode 含 steps Dataset + episode metadata
dataFormat.index: TFDS 内置索引（支持 sharding + deterministic shuffle）
dataFormat.format: TFDS/RLDS（底层 HDF5 或 sharded TFRecord）

changelog.text: 首次发布，包含来自 22 种机器人形态的 60+ 数据集
RT-X 模型发布，数据集扩展至 100万+ 轨迹

usage.load:
import tensorflow_datasets as tfds
ds = tfds.load('fractal20220817_data', split='train')
for episode in ds.take(1):
    for step in episode['steps']:
        img = step['observation']['image']
        action = step['action']
        lang = step['observation'].get('natural_language_instruction', b'').decode()

Collected using a low-cost WidowX robot arm across diverse tabletop manipulation scenes, covering fundamental skills: grasping, placing, pushing, pulling, and stacking.

Widely used in few-shot imitation learning, diffusion policy, and robot foundation model training. High data quality and rich task diversity make it one of the most widely used manipulation learning datasets.

name: AGIBOT WORLD 2026
scale: 数百小时真机数据（五阶段持续发布），100+ 场景，精灵 G2 人形机器人平台
notes: AGIBOT WORLD 2026 real robot dataset. 100% real-world + teleoperation collection on Sprite G2 platform. Multi-modal (RGB-D + tactile + LiDAR + force). Two-phase release (imitation learning + rich interaction). Industry-first dataset focused on physical interaction.
description:
**AGIBOT WORLD 2026** is a large-scale embodied AI dataset officially open-sourced by AGIBOT in April 2026, positioned as the first open data foundation covering the full spectrum of embodied AI research, released in five phases.

**Hardware Platform**: Built on AGIBOT's in-house **Sprite G2** general-purpose humanoid robot, equipped with high-performance joint actuators, multi-modal sensors (multi-view RGB-D, tactile, LiDAR, IMU, force), and a high-performance domain controller.

**Collection**: 100% real-world scenarios + human teleoperation. Uses first-person over-the-horizon teleoperation + Whole-Body Control (WBC), with operators controlling from the robot's perspective. Force-controlled collection captures contact dynamics and force feedback.

**Scenes**: Home, hospitality, commercial spaces, security, industrial logistics — complex real environments with occlusions, backlighting, dynamic crowds, and other real-world noise.

**Two Phases Released**:
- Phase 1 (April 2026): Imitation learning data, hundreds of hours of real-world data with task descriptions, action sequences, atomic skill labels, object annotations, and preserved error recovery trajectories
- Phase 2 (June 3, 2026): **Industry-first open-source dataset focused on physical interaction**, targeting world models and neural simulators. Actively preserves 'failure' data — grasp failures, object slips, collisions, liquid spills, cloth deformation. Experiments confirm that including failure and diverse interaction data significantly improves world model physical consistency

**Ecosystem**: Genie Sim 3.0 simulator + GE-Sim 2.0 world model + GO-2 foundation model + 1:1 digital twin simulation data released in parallel.

**Download**: https://huggingface.co/datasets/agibot-world/AgiBotWorld2026
Project page: https://agibot-world.com

name: Fourier ActionNet (GR-1 Real Robot Dataset)
scale: 30K+ real robot teleoperation trajectories (~140 hours of interaction data)
notes: Fourier GR-1 real robot dataset (ActionNet), 30K+ teleoperation trajectories under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Bimanual dexterous grasping, cabinet opening, material pouring, and more. Compatible with LeRobot, ACT, DP, iDP3.
description:
**Fourier ActionNet (GR-1 Real Robot Dataset)** is Fourier Intelligence's officially open-sourced full-size humanoid robot manipulation dataset, released in **March 2025**.

**Scale**: **30K+ real robot teleoperation trajectories** (~140 hours of interaction data), covering GR-1 (GR1-T1/T2) and GR-2 models.

**Collection**: Apple Vision Pro as the teleoperation master device, with OAK-D W 97 cameras providing first-person perspective. Data format: **HDF5 (joint states) + MP4 (RGB video) + MKV (depth video)**.

**Annotation & Tasks**: Auto-annotated by Qwen2.5-VL-7B vision-language model with manual secondary verification. Covered tasks: pick-and-place, pouring, cabinet opening, precise placement, noisy manipulation, etc.

**Training Frameworks**: Compatible with **LeRobot, ACT, DP, iDP3** and other mainstream frameworks, with full pipeline toolchain (collection → annotation → training → deployment).

**License**: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (non-commercial open source), training/deployment tools under Apache 2.0. Teleoperation source: https://github.com/FFTAI/teleoperation

NVIDIA has also released a GR-1 simulation dataset series — see 'NVIDIA GR-1 Simulation Datasets' entry. Commercial licensing: gr_opensource@fftai.com. Full-body walking data not yet fully released.

name: Unitree LAFAN1 Retargeting Dataset
notes: Unitree's official open-source full-body motion dataset. LAFAN1 mocap data retargeted to H1/H1-2/G1 via Interaction Mesh + IK (Pinocchio). 30FPS CSV joint configurations. Training basis for 2025 Spring Festival Gala dance performance.
scale: 3 humanoid robots (H1 19 joints / H1-2 25 joints / G1 29 joints), LAFAN1 mocap retargeting, 30FPS CSV
description:
Unitree Robotics released this open-source full-body motion dataset in **late 2024**, retargeting Ubisoft's LAFAN1 motion capture data onto **H1 (19 joints), H1-2 (25 joints), and G1 (29 joints)** humanoid robots using numerical optimization (Interaction Mesh + Inverse Kinematics).

**Technical Method**: Motion retargeting powered by the **Pinocchio** rigid-body dynamics library (Maestro Consortium), with constraints including end-effector pose, joint position/velocity limits, and foot-slip prevention. Note: retargeting only accounts for kinematic constraints — no dynamics or actuator limits — so real hardware deployment requires whole-body control (MPC or RL).

**Data Format**: **30FPS CSV files**, each row storing one frame's joint configuration: root joint (XYZ + quaternion) followed by individual joint angles. Includes dance, kicking, and other motion sequences.

**Tools**: Visualization script (rerun_visualize.py) provided, using Pinocchio + **rerun-sdk**. License: motion data **CC BY-NC-ND 4.0**, tools **MIT**.

**Downloads**:
- Official: https://huggingface.co/datasets/unitreerobotics/LAFAN1_Retargeting_Dataset
- Mirror: https://huggingface.co/datasets/lvhaidong/LAFAN1_Retargeting_Dataset
- ModelScope: https://www.modelscope.cn/datasets/AI-ModelScope/LAFAN1_Retargeting_Dataset

**Impact**: Used as the training foundation for Unitree's **2025 Spring Festival Gala** robot dance performance. The Stanford paper **Retargeting Matters** (arXiv:2510.02252, 2025) systematically evaluates retargeting quality's impact on RL policy training, using this dataset as a high-quality baseline. Project page: https://jaraujo98.github.io/retargeting_matters

dataFormat.schema: episode → frame → joint_configuration（root + hip + knee + ankle + shoulder + elbow + wrist）, whole-body 23-DoF

**UnifoLM-WBT (Whole-Body Teleoperation) Dataset** is Unitree's flagship G1 humanoid robot dataset released in **March 2026**, marking the first time a major humanoid robot manufacturer has fully open-sourced high-quality whole-body teleoperation data.

**Scale**: As of March 2026, **340+ hours** of real robot data, **1.89 million motion trajectories**, with ongoing high-frequency updates.

**Key Distinction**: Emphasizes **whole-body coordination** — simultaneously recording bipedal walking, center-of-mass dynamic adjustment, and fine fingertip manipulation, rather than splitting upper/lower body data collection. This is extremely rare among humanoid datasets and directly supports whole-body VLA model training.

**Scenarios**: Real home and industrial open environments — clothing organization, appliance operation, tableware sorting, and other daily tasks.

- UnifoLM-WBT-Dataset (Mar 2026): This dataset, real robot whole-body teleoperation

notes: Standardizes 6 open-source datasets (OXE, OXE-AugE, AgiBot-Beta, RoboCoin, RoboMind, Galaxea) into a unified delta-pose + rotation-vector action space with dual-arm padding. ⚠️ Do not confuse with Tsinghua UniAct (2025 framework) — UniACT is a dataset by AMAP CV Lab.

UniACT's core contribution is **unified action representation**: all heterogeneous data is converted to end-effector delta pose + rotation vector, padded to a standard dual-arm format, enabling cross-embodiment VLA model pre-training. This dataset is the training foundation of the ABot-M0 model.

name: OXE-AugE
scale: 4.4M+ trajectories, 9 robot embodiments
notes: Large-scale cross-embodiment augmentation of OXE. Cross-Painting renders real trajectories onto 9 virtual robots with visual + kinematic fidelity, designed for cross-embodiment generalization.
description:
**OXE-AugE (Open X-Embodiment Augmented Embodiments)** is a large-scale derivative dataset released in late 2025, applying cross-morphology data augmentation to Google's Open X-Embodiment.

**Augmentation Method**: 16 major subsets are selected from the original OXE (1M trajectories). Using **Cross-Painting + AugE-Toolkit**, each trajectory is migrated and rendered onto other robots, ensuring kinematically valid and visually realistic results.

**Scale**: Each original trajectory generates 8 cross-embodiment virtual trajectories + 1 original, totaling **4.4M+ trajectories**, covering Franka, UR5, xArm, WidowX, Google Robot, Jaco, Sawyer, Kinova Gen3, KUKA iiwa — **9 robot morphologies** in total.

**Impact**: The top 4 robots account for 85% of trajectories in the original OXE; AugE enforces balanced distribution, greatly improving diversity. Unified RLDS format, directly loadable with TFDS. Experiments show that policies trained with AugE significantly outperform those trained on real data alone for unseen robot generalization.

dataFormat.layout: HuggingFace LeRobot 格式；按 augmented dataset 分子集仓库

name: DROID
institution: Stanford / Google / UC Berkeley et al. (18 labs)
scale: 76K trajectories, 564 scenes, 350 interaction hours
notes: 18-lab collaboration, Franka Panda unified platform, RLDS format compatible with OXE. Policy generalization improves ~20%.
description:
**DROID (Distributed Robot Interaction Dataset)** is a large-scale real-world robot manipulation dataset released in March 2024 by a consortium of **18 research institutions** including Stanford, Google, and UC Berkeley.

**Collection Scale**: 50 data collectors across **52 buildings and 564 scenes** using a unified Franka Panda robot platform, collecting **76,000+ manipulation trajectories** (350 interaction hours) over 12 months, with ~76K successes + ~16K failures.

**Data Content**: Each trajectory includes 3 camera viewpoints, depth information, camera calibration, and language annotations.

**Format & Impact**: Released in RLDS format (1.7TB), compatible with the Open X-Embodiment ecosystem. Experiments show that co-training with DROID improves policy performance, robustness, and generalization by an average of **~20%**.

dataFormat.schema: episode → step → observation (3× RGB + depth + calibration) / action / language_instruction
dataFormat.layout: RLDS 分片格式；按 recording session 分 episode
dataFormat.index: TFDS 内置索引；episode metadata 含 language_instruction

citation.authors: Alexander Khazatsky, Karl Pertsch et al. (18 institutions)

changelog.text: Initial release: 76K trajectories, 564 scenes, 350h interaction data

usage.load:
# DROID RLDS 格式
import tensorflow_datasets as tfds
ds = tfds.load('droid', split='train')
for episode in ds.take(1):
    for step in episode['steps']:
        img = step['observation']['image']
        action = step['action']
        lang = step['observation']['language_instruction']

name: MimicGen
scale: 48K+ demonstrations, 12 tasks, 4 robot arms
notes: NVIDIA CoRL 2023. Automatically generates large-scale sim trajectories from few human demos. Cross-robot, cross-object. Hugging Face hosted, robomimic compatible.
description:
**MimicGen** is a data generation system published by NVIDIA Research at **CoRL 2023**, capable of automatically synthesizing large-scale, diverse robot manipulation datasets from a small number of human demonstrations (10 per task) in simulation.

**Data Composition**: Includes source (120 human demos), core (26,000 generated samples, 26 distribution variants), robot (16,000, 4 different robot arms), object (2,000, different objects), and large_interpolation (6,000, high difficulty) — totaling **48,000+ demonstration trajectories**.

Released in HDF5 format on Hugging Face, compatible with the **robomimic** framework for BC, BC-RNN, BC-Transformer, and other imitation learning algorithms.

dataContent.tasks: 12 manipulation tasks (pick-place, stacking, coffee, door opening, etc.)

description:
**RoboCasa** is a large-scale home task simulation framework and dataset released by UT Austin and NVIDIA Research at **RSS 2024**.

**Scene Scale**: Built on MuJoCo physics engine + NVIDIA Omniverse rendering, containing **2,500+ 3D assets** (153+ object categories), **120 unique kitchen scenes** (10 floor plans × 12 architectural styles), **100 tasks** (25 atomic + 75 LLM-generated composite tasks like brewing coffee, meal prep, cleaning).

**Data Generation**: Uses MimicGen to automatically generate **100K+ trajectories** from a small number of human demonstrations, supporting mobile manipulators and humanoids.

Generative AI-assisted (Luma.ai for 3D objects, MidJourney for textures and domain randomization). Trained policies can sim-to-real transfer to real robots.

name: ARNOLD
institution: UCLA / Peking Univ / Tsinghua Univ / BIGAI
scale: 8 language-conditioned tasks, 40 objects, 20 scenes, ~10K expert demos
notes: ICCV 2023 / CVPR 2024 Embodied AI Workshop. First benchmark for continuous-goal language grounding. NVIDIA Isaac Sim.
description:
**ARNOLD** (A Benchmark for Language-Grounded Task Learning With Continuous States in Realistic 3D Scenes) is a language-conditioned manipulation benchmark published at **ICCV 2023** with a challenge track at CVPR 2024 Embodied AI Workshop.

**Key Distinction**: Unlike traditional discrete goals (open/close), ARNOLD focuses on **continuous target states** (e.g., open a drawer 50%, lift a bottle to a specific height, rotate to a specified angle), better reflecting real manipulation requirements.

**Benchmark Scale**: 20 photorealistic 3D indoor scenes, 40 objects, 8 language-conditioned tasks (pick-and-place, rotation, drawer/cabinet opening, water pouring/transferring) built in NVIDIA Isaac Sim, with **~10K expert demonstrations** and template-generated language instructions.

Benchmark evaluation reveals that state-of-the-art language-conditioned policy models still face significant challenges in generalizing to unseen scenes, objects, and target states.

dataFormat.layout: HDF5 格式；按 episode 分文件
dataFormat.index: HDF5 内部 attribute；language_goal 为索引键

citation.authors: Ran Gong, Jiangyong Huang et al. (UCLA/PKU/Tsinghua/BIGAI)

description:
**RoboNet** is a large-scale multi-robot interaction dataset jointly released by UC Berkeley, Stanford, UPenn, and CMU, published at **CoRL 2019** and dubbed the 'ImageNet for Robotics'.

**Scale**: **15M+ video frames** from 7 different robot platforms and 113 camera viewpoints, totaling **162,417 examples**.

**Format**: Released in TFDS format (full ~36GB), available in 64×64 and 128×128 resolutions.

The core insight is learning generalizable physical world dynamics priors through large-scale, cross-robot, cross-scene video prediction, advancing robot learning from single-task to general-purpose manipulation capabilities.

institution: Meta AI / UT Austin / CMU et al. (13 universities)
scale: 3,670 hours egocentric video, 931 wearers, 74 locations
notes: CVPR 2022. World's largest egocentric video dataset. 5 benchmarks (episodic memory, hand-object, audio-visual conversation, social, forecasting). Requires data use agreement.
description:
**Ego4D** is the world's largest first-person (egocentric) video dataset and benchmark, released by **Meta AI (FAIR)** together with **13 universities** worldwide at **CVPR 2022**.

**Collection Scale**: 931 camera wearers across 9 countries and 74 locations, capturing **3,670 hours** of continuous daily video (~7TB) with **3.8M narrative text pairs**.

**Five Benchmark Tasks**: Episodic Memory, Hand-Object Manipulation, Audio-Visual Conversation, Social Interaction, and Activity Forecasting — directly mapping to robot perception, planning, and interaction capabilities.

Companion **EgoVLP** (NeurIPS 2022) pre-training framework. Download requires signing a data use agreement on the official website.

name: HANDAL (Household ANnotated Dataset for functionAL grasping)
scale: 308K 标注帧 + 2.2K 视频，212 个真实物体 + 17 类别
notes: NVIDIA IROS 2023，首个类别级 6-DoF 位姿 + 可供性标注的真实物体数据集。提供像素级 3D handle affordance 分割、物体 6-DoF 位姿、2D/3D 重建网格。AFUN 在 HANDAL-Mini/Easy/Hard 三个测试集上取得 SOTA。
description:
**HANDAL（Household ANnotated Dataset for functionAL grasping）**是 NVIDIA 于 IROS 2023 发布的可供性分割基准数据集。

**规模**：**30.8 万** 标注帧、**2.2K** 视频序列、**212** 个真实世界物体，覆盖 **17 个类别**（工具箱类：锤子、钳子、螺丝刀、扳手等；厨房类：铲子、量杯、滤网等）。

**标注内容**：类别级 6-DoF 位姿 + 尺度、像素级物体分割、**像素级 3D handle affordance 分割**、2D 边界框、3D 物体网格重建。

**测试拆分**：HANDAL-Mini / HANDAL Easy / HANDAL Hard 三个测试集，分别测试模型在不同难度下的可供性分割能力。

**协议**：CC BY-NC-SA 4.0。

AFUN 论文在 HANDAL 三个测试集上全面超越所有基线（+23.9 gIoU）。

**3DOI（3D Object Interaction）**是密歇根大学于 ICCV 2023 提出的 3D 物体交互理解数据集。

**规模**：约 **1 万张** RGB 图像，来源涵盖互联网视频、第一人称视频及 3D 数据集渲染。

**标注内容**：交互查询点、可供性关键点、2D 边界框、关节类型（旋转/平移/固定）、可移动性、夹具标签。

name: HOVA-500K (Human-Object Visual Affordance 500K)
scale: 50 万张 RGB 图像 + 1,726 物体类别 + 675 动作类别
notes: HKUST 2025 (GLOVER++)，当前最大规模的视觉可供性数据集。从 Ego4D/EPIC-KITCHENS 等人类视频自动提取手-物接触帧并回投标注。AFUN 在 HOVA-500K 可供性分割上取得 SOTA，并在其基础上转换生成了分割训练数据。
description:
**HOVA-500K（Human-Object Visual Affordance 500K）**是 HKUST（广州）于 2025 年发布的大规模视觉可供性数据集，伴随 GLOVER++ 模型。

**规模**：**50 万** 张 RGB 图像，覆盖 **1,726** 个物体类别和 **675** 个动作/动词类别，是目前最大规模的 visual affordance 数据集。

**采集方式**：从 Ego4D、EPIC-KITCHENS 等第一人称人类视频中检测手-物接触帧，通过 homography 反投影将接触点映射到无遮挡帧，实现毫米级交互点标注。

**标注**：point-level Gaussian heatmap（非区域掩码），以接触点为中心的高斯热力图，精度可达毫米级。

**关联模型**：GLOVER++（全局-局部可供性训练框架），仅 8.1M 可训练参数，在真实机器人零样本操作中达 73.3% 成功率。

AFUN 将 HOVA-500K 的 point-level 标注转换为分割掩码用于训练，并在其测试集上取得 SOTA。

dataFormat.schema: image → object_category / action_category → contact_heatmap（Gaussian keypoint）
dataFormat.layout: 按 image 分文件；每 PNG 配 NPY 热力图
dataFormat.index: object_category + action_category 双索引

dataContent.scenes: Ego4D/EPIC-KITCHENS 等第一人称视频中的日常生活场景
dataContent.tasks: 视觉可供性关键点预测、可供性分割、接触点预测

quality.collection: 多源聚合（Ego4D/EPIC-KITCHENS 自动标注）

name: ReasonAFF (Reasoning-based Affordance Dataset)
scale: 48 物体类别 + 30 可供性类别 + GPT-4o 推理指令重写
notes: 上海 AI Lab 2025 (Affordance-R1)，基于 InstructPart 扩展的推理级可供性数据集。将直接指令改写为隐含推理问题（如 '打开垃圾桶应该碰哪里？'），考验 MLLM 的 Chain-of-Thought 可供性推理。AFUN 在 ReasonAFF 上大幅超越 Seg-Zero/Vision Reasoner/Qwen2.5VL 等基线。
description:
**ReasonAFF** 是上海 AI Lab 于 2025 年伴随 Affordance-R1 模型发布的推理级可供性数据集。

**数据来源**：基于 InstructPart 数据集，通过 GPT-4o 将原来的直接指令（如 'segment the lid'）改写为隐含推理问题（如 'Where would you place your hand if you wanted to open this trash can?'）。

**规模**：覆盖 **48** 个物体类别、**30** 个可供性类别，每条数据包含推理指令 + 可供性分割掩码 + 边界框 + 中心点。

**核心创新**：将可供性分割从纯视觉任务升级为视觉-语言推理任务，需要模型先理解任务语义再定位操作部位。

**关联模型**：Affordance-R1，采用纯强化学习（GRPO）训练，零样本泛化到 AGD20K/UMD 等 OOD 数据集。

AFUN 在 ReasonAFF 上取得 gIoU 67.41 / cIoU 62.72 的 SOTA 成绩。

dataFormat.index: instruction-annotation JSON 配对索引

name: InstructPart
scale: 2,400 真实图像 + 48 物体类别 + 9,600 任务指令
notes: CMU ACL 2025 Main，首个桥接任务导向交互与部件分割的基准。含 9,600 条人工标注任务指令（TRPS 任务推理部件分割 + ORPS 指代部件分割）。AFUN 在 InstructPart 可供性分割上取得 SOTA。
description:
**InstructPart** 是 CMU 机器人研究所于 ACL 2025 (Main) 提出的任务导向部件分割基准。

**规模**：**2,400** 张真实世界图像，覆盖 **48** 个物体类别、**44** 个部件类别、**30** 个可供性类别、**37** 个动作类别。

**标注**：**9,600** 条人工标注任务指令（含专家标注 + GPT-polished + 人工核验），像素级分割掩码。

**两大任务**：
1. **TRPS（Task Reasoning Part Segmentation）**——给定任务指令+图像，推理并分割出正确操作部件（如 '拉开微波炉需要碰哪个部位？'）
2. **ORPS（Oracle Referring Part Segmentation）**——给定直接部件/物体指代，分割对应部件（如 '水龙头的把手'）

**协议**：CC BY 4.0。

AFUN 在 InstructPart 上与 ReasonAFF 一起作为核心可供性分割评测基准。

dataFormat.schema: image → task_instruction → part_segmentation_mask / affordance_label / object_category
dataFormat.layout: 按 image 分文件；每图配 JSON 任务指令 + 分割标注
dataFormat.index: task_instruction + part_category JSON 索引
dataFormat.format: PNG（RGB）+ JSON（任务指令 + 分割标注）

dataContent.objects: 48 类日常物体（微波炉、水龙头、垃圾桶、剪刀等）
dataContent.tasks: TRPS（任务推理部件分割）+ ORPS（指代部件分割）

name: SceneFun3D
scale: 710 个高精度 3D 室内场景 + 14.8K+ 交互标注 + 3,000+ 任务描述
notes: ETH/Microsoft CVPR 2024 Oral，首个精细 3D 场景功能理解数据集。Faro 激光扫描仪捕捉 710 个真实室内场景，标注了把手、旋钮、按钮、开关等细小可交互元素。AFUN 在 SceneFun3D 的 3D 运动预测（n=721）上取得 SOTA。
description:
**SceneFun3D** 是 ETH Zürich / Google / TUM / Microsoft 联合发表于 CVPR 2024 (Oral) 的精细 3D 场景功能理解数据集。

**规模**：**710** 个高精度 3D 室内场景（Faro 激光扫描仪采集，远超 RGB-D 重建精度），**14,800+** 精细交互标注，**3,000+** 自然语言任务描述，覆盖 **9** 个可供性类别（旋转、钩拉、推按等）。

**独特之处**：标注了传统 RGB-D 重建（ScanNet/Matterport3D）中不可见的细小功能元素——门把手、旋钮、按钮、开关、拨杆等。

**三大基准任务**：
1. **Functionality Segmentation**：3D 点云中分割功能元素并预测可供性标签
2. **Task-Driven Affordance Grounding**：根据自然语言任务描述定位功能元素
3. **3D Motion Estimation**：预测交互所需的运动参数（运动类型、轴、原点）

**协议**：CC BY-NC-SA 4.0。

institution: 北京人形机器人创新中心 / 北京大学

description:
**RoboMIND 2.0** 是北京人形机器人创新中心与北京大学于 2025 年 12 月发布的超大规模多模态真实机器人操作数据集，是 RoboMIND 1.0 (RSS 2025) 的全面升级。

**数据规模**：**31 万+** 演示轨迹、**1,000+** 小时操作数据、**759** 种精细任务、**1,139** 种交互物体、覆盖 **11** 个核心场景（工业分拣、化学实验室、家庭厨房、家电交互等）。

**多形态覆盖**：**6 种机器人形态**——Franka Emika Panda、UR5e、AgileX 轮式双臂平台、'天工'全尺寸人形机器人（含灵巧手）、'天轶'轮式人形机器人、ARX 轮式机器人。

**多模态感知**：
- **视觉**：多视角 RGB-D
- **触觉**：1.2 万条触觉增强片段（法向力 + 切向力），行业首次大规模集成
- **语言**：Gemini 辅助生成的精细任务描述
- **本体感知**：完整关节状态

**训练框架**：配套 MIND-2 分层架构——慢系统（VLM 推理规划）+ 快系统（VLA 实时控制），采用 Implicit Q-Learning 离线 RL 训练。

**协议**：CC BY-NC 4.0。

AFUN 使用 RoboMIND2 作为训练数据源，并将其测试集（n=156）作为域外（out-of-domain）3D 运动评测基准。

**HOI4D（4D Egocentric Human-Object Interaction）**是清华大学与北京大学于 CVPR 2022 发布的大规模 4D 第一人称人-物交互数据集。

**规模**：**240 万** RGB-D 第一人称视频帧、**4,000+** 交互序列、**800** 个物体实例（16 类别，含刚体和铰接物体）、**610** 个不同室内场景。

1. 4D 动态点云序列语义分割
2. 类别级物体位姿跟踪
3. 第一人称动作分割

**协议**：CC BY-NC-SA 4.0。

AFUN 将 HOI4D 作为人类第一人称（human egocentric）训练数据源，从中提取 2,165 个动作区间用于可供性学习。

name: VITRA (Scalable VLA Pretraining with Human Activity Videos)
scale: 1,098,944 动作区间（基于 EPIC-KITCHENS + Ego4D 人类视频）
notes: Microsoft 2025，规模化 VLA 预训练数据集。从 EPIC-KITCHENS 和 Ego4D 人类活动视频中提取操作片段，提供逐帧 SLAM 相机内外参。AFUN 的最大训练数据源（129,433 条拟合曲线）。
description:
**VITRA（Scalable Vision-Language-Action Model Pretraining with Human Activity Videos）**是 Microsoft Research 于 2025 年发布的规模化 VLA 预训练数据集。

**规模**：基于 **EPIC-KITCHENS** 和 **Ego4D** 两大第一人称视频数据集，提取了约 **110 万** 个操作动作区间，累计约 130 万视角观测。

**核心技术**：为第一人称视频提供逐帧 **SLAM 相机内参和外参**，解决了第一人称视频中相机运动噪声的核心挑战——使轨迹可几何投影到 3D 空间。

**标注**：使用 VLM + SAM3 跟踪 → 深度反投影 → Bézier 曲线拟合的自动化管线，从人类操作视频中提取物体运动轨迹。

**协议**：CC BY 4.0。

AFUN 将 VITRA 作为最大的训练数据来源（human egocentric 类别），从中生成了 129,433 条有效 Bézier 样条运动曲线。

name: BC-Z (Berkeley)
institution: Google / Everyday Robots / UC Berkeley / Stanford
scale: 39,350 训练轨迹 + 3,914 验证轨迹，100 种操作任务，81 GB (TFDS)
notes: Google/UC Berkeley 2022，100 种桌面操作任务，全自然语言标注。Franka Panda 单臂，DAgger 方式采集（7 名操作员 × 12 台机器人），是 OpenVLA 等 VLA 模型的核心预训练子集。早期 VLA 标杆数据集。
description:
**BC-Z（BC-Zero）**是 Google Robotics、Everyday Robots、UC Berkeley 和 Stanford 于 2022 年联合发布的大规模机器人模仿学习数据集。

**规模**：**39,350** 条训练轨迹 + **3,914** 条验证轨迹，覆盖 **100 种** 桌面操作任务（抓取、开抽屉、摆物件、倒水等），累计 125 小时机器人操作数据。

**采集方式**：7 名操作员通过 DAgger（Dataset Aggregation）方式远程操控 12 台 Franka Panda 机械臂，交替执行自主策略和遥操作干预。

**数据格式**：TFDS（TensorFlow Datasets），每条轨迹包含：RGB 图像观测 (171×213)、末端执行器 3D 位置残差 + 轴角旋转残差 + 夹爪开合、自然语言任务指令 + 512 维 Universal Sentence Encoder 嵌入。

**核心特性**：零样本任务泛化——通过语言描述或人类操作视频即可执行训练中未见过的任务。是 OpenVLA、RT-1 等 VLA 模型的核心预训练子集。

**协议**：CC BY 4.0。

name: ALOHA Dataset
scale: ALOHA Sim ~5 GB + Mobile ALOHA 50+ GB + ALOHA 2 26K+ 轨迹 + LeRobot 多子集

description:
**ALOHA（A Low-cost Open-source Hardware System for Bimanual Teleoperation）**是 Stanford 于 2023 年提出的低成本双臂遥操作平台，配套数据集已成为精细操作领域的标杆基准。

**平台组成**：
- **ALOHA Stationary**：双 ViperX 300 机械臂（6-DoF + 夹爪），桌面固定
- **Mobile ALOHA**：ALOHA 双臂 + AgileX Tracer 移动底盘，支持移动操作
- **ALOHA 2**（Google DeepMind 2024）：26,000+ 演示轨迹，5 项高难度灵巧任务

**数据集组成**：
- **ALOHA Sim 数据集**（~5 GB，Google Drive）：仿真 Transfer Cube / Bimanual Insertion 任务的脚本演示 + 人类遥操作数据
- **Mobile ALOHA 数据集**（50+ GB，TFDS）：真实场景下 7 类家务任务（炒虾、擦桌子、叠衣服等）
- **LeRobot ALOHA 子集**（HuggingFace）：`lerobot/aloha_static_coffee` 等多个标准化子集

**适配算法**：ACT、Diffusion Policy、VINN、DiT-Policy、π0 等。

**协议**：MIT（代码），CC BY 4.0（数据）。

name: GR00T N1 Official Dataset
scale: 78 万合成轨迹 + 真实遥操作 + 人类视频（Ego4D/Ego-Exo4D）
notes: NVIDIA GTC 2025 发布，全球首个开源人形机器人基础模型 GR00T N1 的训练数据集。含 78 万合成轨迹（约等于 6,500 小时演示）、真实遥操作数据、互联网规模人类视频。含人形行走/搬物/开关家具等多样化任务。混合训练比纯真实数据性能提升 40%。
description: GR00T N1 官方数据集是 NVIDIA 于 GTC 2025 伴随 GR00T N1 模型发布的大规模人形机器人训练数据集。数据金字塔三层：真实遥操作轨迹（顶层，量少质高）跨多机器人平台；合成数据（中层）通过 DexMimicGen + RoboCasa 在 Omniverse 中生成 78 万条合成轨迹（相当于 6,500 小时人工演示）；互联网视频（底层，量最大）Ego4D、Ego-Exo4D 等。合成 + 真实混合训练比纯真实数据性能提升 40%；仅用 10% 数据在 Fourier GR-1 上达 42.6% 成功率，全量数据达 76.8%。协议：Apache 2.0（代码），CC BY 4.0（数据）。与网站已有的 NVIDIA GR-1 仿真数据集互补。

dataFormat.schema: episode -> step -> observation（RGB + proprioception）/ action（full-body joint_targets）/ language_instruction
dataFormat.layout: LeRobot 格式 + HDF5 + MP4；按数据源分层组织
dataFormat.index: LeRobot meta/info.json + 数据源分层元数据
dataFormat.format: LeRobot Parquet v3.0 + HDF5 v2.0 + MP4

dataContent.scenes: 仿真桌面操作 + 真实室内外场景
dataContent.objects: 多样化家具与日常物品（抽屉、微波炉、杯子、工具等）
dataContent.tasks: 人形行走、搬运物体、开关家具、桌面操作、双臂协作
dataContent.episodes: 78 万合成轨迹 + 真实遥操作 + 互联网视频

quality.collection: 多源聚合（真机遥操作 + 合成生成 + 互联网视频）
quality.annotation: 自动标注（VLM + 逆动力学推断）

description: InternHumanoid MotionMillions 是上海 AI 实验室于 2025 年发布的超大规模人形机器人运动数据集。规模：200 万+ 动作序列（是现有人体动作数据集的 20 倍以上），总时长超 2,000 小时，百余种动作类别。动作覆盖：武术（23.7%）、健身（26.4%）、表演（17.5%）、舞蹈（14.9%）、体育（2.4%）等。每个动作配备 20+ 种不同风格语义描述（情绪/体态/风格/环境），叠加 GPT-4o 自动标注，分布多样性提升 200%。配套工具：InternHumanoid 跨本体人形机器人全身运动控制工具链；Scamo7B 文本-动作基础生成模型（7B 参数）。协议：Apache 2.0。

name: GraspVLA / SynGrasp-1B
institution: Galbot / 北京大学 / 港大 / 北京智源
scale: 10 亿帧合成抓取数据 + 10,000+ 物体 + 240 物体类别
notes: Galbot/北大 CoRL 2025，十亿级合成抓取基础模型 GraspVLA 配套数据集 SynGrasp-1B。MuJoCo 物理 + Isaac Sim 光追渲染，100 条微调即达 90% 工业件成功率。透明物体抓取 86.6%（传统方法仅 10%）。LIBERO 长程 94.1% 零样本。
description: GraspVLA / SynGrasp-1B 是 Galbot、北京大学、港大和北京智源于 CoRL 2025 发布的十亿级合成抓取数据集。规模：10 亿帧合成抓取数据，覆盖 10,000+ 物体（240 类别，来自 Objaverse）。生成管线：MuJoCo 物理仿真稳定抓取 + BoDex 反掌抓取生成 + CuRobo 无碰撞轨迹规划 + Isaac Sim 光追真实渲染。关键能力：零样本合成类别 93.3% 真实世界成功率；零样本互联网类别 86.6%；透明物体 86.6%（AnyGrasp 仅 10%）通过 RGB 多视角 + 语义推理绕过深度传感器局限；少样本微调 100 条 demo 即从 30% 提升到 90%。协议：CC BY 4.0。

name: OpenRoboCare
scale: 19.8h, 31,185 samples, 15 caregiving tasks
notes: World's first multi-modal expert demonstration dataset for robot caregiving. 21 OTs, 15 care tasks, 5 sensing modalities. BSD-3-Clause.
description: OpenRoboCare is the world's first multi-modal expert demonstration dataset for robot-assisted caregiving, published at IROS 2025 by Cornell's EmPRISE Lab. 21 licensed occupational therapists perform 15 core caregiving tasks on two hospital manikins across five ADL categories. Five modalities: RGB-D (3x RealSense), 12-camera OptiTrack motion capture, Pupil Labs eye-tracking (120Hz), 44-point tactile skin (60Hz), and expert-labeled hierarchical temporal annotations. 315 sessions, 19.8 hours, 31,185 samples. BSD-3-Clause. AWS Open Data.

name: ETRI-Activity3D-LivingLab
institution: ETRI (Electronics and Telecommunications Research Institute, Korea)
scale: 112,620 samples, 55 action classes, 100 subjects
notes: 55 elderly daily activity classes with Kinect RGB-D and 3D skeleton. Real home environments. For elderly-care robot action recognition.
description: ETRI-Activity3D is a large-scale RGB-D dataset for elderly daily activity recognition by ETRI Korea (IROS 2020), with the LivingLab extension (ROMAN 2021). First 3D action recognition dataset in actual elderly homes. Kinect v2: RGB (1920x1080), depth, 25-joint 3D skeleton. 112,620 samples, 100 subjects (50 elderly + 50 young), 55 action classes covering eating, cooking, hygiene, dressing, household chores, social interaction, exercise, and safety-critical actions. LivingLab targets domain shift for real-world deployment.

name: KFall
institution: KAIST (Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology)
scale: 32 subjects, 15 fall types + 21 ADLs, 100Hz IMU
notes: First open dataset for pre-impact fall detection. 9-axis IMU at waist, 100Hz with 90fps synchronized video for fall timestamp labeling. DL benchmark: 99.3% sensitivity.
description: KFall is the first publicly available dataset for pre-impact fall detection (Frontiers in Aging Neuroscience, 2021). 32 Korean young adults, LPMS-B2 9-axis IMU at waist (100Hz), synchronized Raspberry Pi HQ camera (90fps). 15 simulated fall types + 21 ADLs. CNN-LSTM: 99.32% sensitivity / 99.01% specificity. Later validated on FARSEEING real elderly falls. Key contribution: synchronized video-based fall moment temporal labels enabling pre-impact detection.

dataFormat.schema: subject → trial → accelerometer(3) / gyroscope(3) / magnetometer(3) / euler(3) × timestamp(100Hz)
dataFormat.layout: 按受试者和动作类型分目录，同步视频单独存放
dataFormat.index: 提供 trial_label.csv 标注动作类型和跌倒时刻（t_fall）

sensors: Raspberry Pi HQ 相机（90fps 同步视频）

changelog.text: 首次发布，Frontiers in Aging Neuroscience 发表

usage.training: 适用于 CNN-LSTM、Transformer、SVM 等时序分类模型。重点是跌倒前 300-500ms 窗口的特征提取。

Code: github.com/Xiaofeng-Han-Res/HMR-1.

name: Language Table
scale: 600K 语言标注轨迹，多种几何形状积木
notes: 600K language-labeled trajectories, open-vocabulary multi-task tabletop manipulation. OXE member, widely used in VLA training.
description:
Language-Table is an open-vocabulary, multi-task tabletop manipulation benchmark published by **Google Research**.

It contains **600K** language-labeled robot trajectories covering diverse geometric block pushing tasks. The robot is controlled via natural language instructions in real-time, supporting open-vocabulary interactive language-driven control.

It is a member of **Open X-Embodiment** and widely used by Octo, OpenVLA, RDT-1B, and other mainstream VLA models.

dataFormat.schema: episode → step → observation / action / language_instruction
dataFormat.layout: RLDS 格式，每 episode 含 RGB 图像 + 语言指令 + 动作序列
dataFormat.index: TFDS 内置索引

name: Furniture Bench
scale: 550+ 小时人类演示，9 款 IKEA 风格家具
notes: Long-horizon furniture assembly benchmark, 5+ IKEA models, 550h human data. OXE member. Imitation learning ≤42% success rate, exposing limitations of current methods.
description:
FurnitureBench is a real-world long-horizon furniture assembly benchmark published by **UT Austin** and **NVIDIA Research**.

It features **5+ IKEA-style** 3D-printed furniture models and over **550 hours** of human teleoperation demonstrations. Tasks involve grasping, insertion, and screwing — fine-grained long-horizon manipulation. It serves as a standard benchmark for evaluating long-sequence robot manipulation capability.

State-of-the-art imitation learning achieves ≤42% success, exposing significant shortcomings of current approaches.

dataFormat.schema: episode → step → observation / action / reward
dataFormat.layout: RLDS 格式，多视角 RGB-D + 本体状态 + 动作序列
dataFormat.index: TFDS 内置索引

dataContent.tasks: 9 种家具装配任务（抓取、插入、拧螺丝）

benchmarks.conditions: 9 家具全装配任务，低随机初始化
行为克隆 ResNet-18，9 家具全装配

name: VIMA
scale: 600K+ 专家轨迹，多模态 prompt，4 级泛化
notes: VIMA-BENCH: multimodal prompt tabletop manipulation benchmark, 600K trajectories. Supports text/image/video mixed prompts, 4-tier generalization protocol for zero-shot evaluation.
description:
VIMA-BENCH is a large-scale multimodal robot learning benchmark published by **Stanford** and **NVIDIA**.

It includes **600K+** expert demonstration trajectories across procedurally generated tabletop manipulation tasks, supporting text, image, and video mixed prompts. It defines a **4-tier generalization protocol** (placement / combinatorial / novel object / novel task) to evaluate zero-shot generalization.

The VIMA model uses a Transformer architecture to directly process multimodal tokens, significantly outperforming prior methods on visual reasoning and imitation learning tasks.

dataFormat.schema: episode → step → observation / action / prompt
dataFormat.layout: HDF5 格式，每 episode 含 RGB 图像 + 多模态 prompt + 动作序列
dataFormat.index: 内置 episode 索引

dataContent.tasks: 6 类任务（rearrangement / one-shot imitation 等）
annotations: 多模态 prompt（文本+图像+视频）

name: QT-Opt
scale: 580K+ 真实抓取试验，7 台 Kuka 机器人
notes: Classic Kuka grasping dataset, 580K+ real-world trials, 96% grasp success rate. Distributed deep RL across 7 Kuka robots. Landmark work in scalable real-world RL.
description:
QT-Opt is a landmark robot grasping dataset published by **Google** and **UC Berkeley**.

It contains over **580K** real-world grasping trials collected by **7 Kuka robots** over months of continuous operation. Using distributed deep reinforcement learning, it achieves a **96%** grasp success rate and generalizes to unseen objects.

This work demonstrated the feasibility of large-scale real-world RL training and remains a foundational reference in robot learning.

name: Stanford HYDRA
scale: 多任务家用操作（咖啡、烤面包、摆盘）
notes: Hybrid action space imitation learning: sparse waypoints + dense low-level actions. OXE member. Coffee/toast/dish sorting tasks. Reduces distribution shift by 40%.
description:
Stanford HYDRA is a household robot manipulation dataset published by **Stanford University**.

It collects teleoperation demonstrations for multi-task household scenarios including **coffee making**, **toast making**, and **dish sorting**. HYDRA uses a hybrid action space (sparse waypoints + dense low-level actions) with dynamic switching and action relabeling to reduce distribution shift by **40%**.

It is a member of the **Open X-Embodiment** dataset collection.

name: TidyBot
scale: LLM 驱动的个性化家居整理，85% 真实世界成功率
notes: LLM-powered personalized household tidying. Infers user preferences from few-shot examples, 85% real-world success. Mobile manipulation robot.
description:
TidyBot is an LLM-powered personalized household tidying dataset published by **Princeton University**.

The system uses large language models to infer personalized tidying preferences from few-shot user examples, controlling a mobile manipulation robot to place items in appropriate receptacles. It achieves **85%** task success rate in real-world testing.

This is a representative application of LLM + robot manipulation, demonstrating the reasoning and generalization capabilities of language models in physical-world tasks.

name: Columbia PushT Dataset
scale: 15 个任务，Diffusion Policy 标准 benchmark
notes: Standard Diffusion Policy benchmark. Push T-block to target pose → exit zone. 15 task variants. 46.9% higher success rate achieved.
description:
The Columbia PushT Dataset is a precision manipulation dataset published by **Columbia University**, serving as the standard benchmark for **Diffusion Policy**.

It contains **15 task variants**, with the core task being pushing a T-shaped block to a target pose and then moving to an exit zone. Diffusion Policy uses iterative denoising to generate action sequences, achieving **46.9%** higher success rate with real-time inference.

This dataset has become the de facto standard for evaluating diffusion-based generative manipulation policies.

name: DobbE
scale: 109 个真实家庭，7 类任务，81% 成功率
notes: Decentralized home robot data. 109 real homes, 7 task types. Learns a task in 20 minutes, 81% success. Open-source hardware design + data.
description:
DobbE is a decentralized home robot learning dataset published by **NYU** and **Meta AI**.

It collects data across **109 real homes** for 7 types of household tasks (door/drawer opening/closing, handle grasping, pick-and-place, random play) using a custom low-cost collection tool (the Stick). DobbE uses adaptive pretrained representations (HPR) to learn new tasks in just **20 minutes**, achieving **81%** success rate.

The project open-sources data, models, and hardware designs to democratize home robotics.

name: ALFRED
scale: 8055 专家演示，25743 条指令，模拟家居多步任务
notes: Classic simulated household instruction-following benchmark. 8,055 expert demos + 25,743 NL instructions. Avg 50 steps per task. First-person vision.
description:
ALFRED (Action Learning From REalistic Directions) is a classic simulated household task instruction dataset published by **USC** and **Allen Institute for AI**.

It contains **8,055** expert demonstrations and **25,743** high/low-level natural language instructions covering multi-step tasks in simulated home environments, averaging **50 action steps** per task. Using first-person visual input, models must map vision and language instructions to action sequences.

ALFRED is a classic benchmark in embodied instruction following, widely used for evaluating long-horizon planning in VLN and VLA models.

name: BEHAVIOR-1K
notes: Large-scale daily activity simulation benchmark. 1,000 activities (effortful to enjoyable), 50+ scenes, 5,000+ annotated objects with physics and semantics.
description:
BEHAVIOR-1K is a large-scale daily activity simulation benchmark published by **Stanford University**.

Through a human needs survey, it collected **1,000 activities** that people most want robots to assist with (effortful activities ranked highest, enjoyable ranked lowest). It features **50+ scenes** and **5,000+** objects annotated with physics and semantic properties.

The benchmark aims to advance general-purpose home robots and is one of the largest and most comprehensive daily activity simulation benchmarks to date.

name: CMU Play Fusion
scale: 3 个复杂场景（烧烤/摆桌/洗碗）
notes: OXE member. Unstructured play data for skill discovery: grill/table/dishwasher. Diffusion model + discrete skill bottleneck.
description:
CMU Play Fusion is an unstructured robot manipulation dataset published by **Carnegie Mellon University**.

It collects data by letting a robot freely play in **3 complex scenes** (grill, dining table, dishwasher), then uses a diffusion model with a discrete skill bottleneck to discover interpretable and generalizable skills from unstructured play data.

It is a member of **Open X-Embodiment**.

name: NYU Franka Play
scale: 玩具厨房自由操作，微波炉/烤箱/灶台
notes: OXE member, Play-to-Policy. Converts unstructured human play into goal-conditioned policies. Zero-shot generalization. Toy kitchen.
description:
NYU Franka Play is a Play-to-Policy dataset published by **New York University**.

It collects unstructured play data by having humans freely operate a Franka robot in a toy kitchen, then converts it into goal-conditioned policies enabling zero-shot generalization.

It is a member of **Open X-Embodiment**, with core scenes including microwave/oven door, stove knobs, and pot manipulation.

Berkeley Autolab UR5 is an adaptive robot manipulation research platform dataset published by **UC Berkeley**.

It collects data on **4 manipulation tasks** using a UR5 robot: simple pick-and-place, cloth wiping, cup stacking, and bottle pick-and-place requiring precise 6DoF rotation. It focuses on deformable object handling and human-robot collaboration.

It is a member of **Open X-Embodiment**.

Berkeley Fanuc Manipulation is an industrial robot manipulation dataset published by **UC Berkeley**.

It uses a **FANUC Mate 200iD** industrial robot to collect multi-view video, proprioceptive data, and language instructions. Tasks include drawer opening/closing, object grasping, door closing, computer closing, and object pushing.

It is a member of **Open X-Embodiment**.

name: Berkeley Cable Routing
scale: 线缆穿夹，分层模仿学习，自主故障恢复
notes: Hierarchical imitation learning for cable routing through clips. Autonomous failure recovery. Interactive fine-tuning for new scenarios.
description:
Berkeley Cable Routing is a precision manipulation dataset published by **UC Berkeley**.

It trains a robot to route cable through tightly-fitting clips using a hierarchical imitation learning framework — high-level sequencing of reusable visuomotor primitives and low-level precise execution. The system autonomously recovers from failures and adapts to new scenarios via interactive fine-tuning.

name: CMU Stretch
scale: VRB 视觉可供性，从人类视频迁移技能到机器人
notes: OXE member. VRB learns visual affordances from human videos for cross-task robot policy transfer. 10+ real-world tasks with minimal demos.
description:
CMU Stretch is a visual affordance robot manipulation dataset published by **Carnegie Mellon University**.

Based on the VRB (Vision-Robotics Bridge) framework, it learns visual affordances from human videos and transfers this knowledge to robot policies. Using a Hello Robot Stretch, it validates on **10+ real household tasks** with minimal demonstrations.

It is a member of **Open X-Embodiment**.

notes: Crowdsourced teleoperation: 2,144 real Sawyer demos across 3 tasks (laundry, tower building, search). Scalable imitation learning.
description:
RoboTurk is a crowdsourced robot teleoperation dataset published by **Stanford University**.

It collected **2,144** real Sawyer robot teleoperation demonstrations via the RoboTurk crowdsourcing platform, covering **3 tasks**: flattening laundry, building towers from bowls, and searching for objects.

It demonstrated the feasibility of large-scale crowdsourced robot data collection for imitation learning.

name: RoboMimic
scale: 模拟+真实多阶段操作，离线 IL/RL benchmark
notes: Open-source human-demo robot manipulation benchmark. Multi-stage tasks in sim+real. Standard benchmark for offline IL and RL algorithms.
description:
RoboMimic is a robot manipulation imitation learning benchmark dataset published by **Stanford University**.

It provides high-quality human demonstration trajectories for multi-stage manipulation tasks in both **simulation and real-world** settings. It includes multiple difficulty levels (lift / can / square / transport) and supports both single-arm and bimanual operation.

RoboMimic has become one of the most widely used benchmarks in offline robot learning.

MIME (CMU) is an early human-robot interaction demonstration dataset published by **Carnegie Mellon University**.

It contains **8,260** human-robot interaction demonstrations covering **20** different manipulation tasks, from simple object pushing to complex household item stacking. Each demo includes video, state, and action data.

notes: Hand-held data collection device with synchronized audio+visual feedback. Learns contact-rich manipulation policies from human demos.
description:
ManiWAV is a hand-held robot data collection device and dataset published by **Stanford University** and **Columbia University**.

It synchronously records **audio** and **visual** feedback during human manipulation demonstrations — audio signals provide rich information about contact state, material properties, and task progress. The dataset supports training audio-visual robot manipulation policies, especially for contact-rich tasks.

RoboTwin 2.0 is a simulated bimanual manipulation benchmark published by **ACE Robotics** and **CUHK**.

It contains **100K+** bimanual manipulation trajectories across **50 tasks**, using the RoboTwin-OD object dataset spanning **147 categories** and **731 unique objects** with rich semantic annotations.

It is from the same team as ACE-Ego, focusing on standardized evaluation of bimanual coordination.

RoboVQA is an RGB-D scene graph dataset published by **Stanford University**.

It converts RGB-D inputs into actionable scene graphs, enabling robots to understand and execute long-horizon user requests across **3 office buildings**. Through scene graph reasoning, it achieves **89%** real-world success rate for language-guided task execution.

Something-Something V2 is a large-scale human action understanding video dataset published by **TwentyBN**.

It contains **220,847** videos covering **174** fine-grained action categories (e.g., 'picking something up', 'putting something down', 'pushing something'). Each video shows human hands performing basic object manipulation actions.

⚠️ **Note: This is human activity data, not a robot dataset.** It is frequently used by the VLA community for visual representation pretraining to help models understand basic manipulation semantics.

YouCook2 is a task-oriented cooking instructional video dataset published by **University of Michigan**.

It contains **2,000** long untrimmed videos covering **89** cooking recipes. Each video shows a human completing a full dish from start to finish, with natural language narration and action sequences.

⚠️ **Note: This is human activity data, not a robot dataset.** It is commonly used for long-sequence task planning and procedural understanding research.

100DOH (100 Days of Hands) is a large-scale hand-object interaction video dataset published by **University of Michigan**.

It collects **27.3K** YouTube videos spanning 11 categories of daily interactions, with nearly **131 days** of interaction footage. Annotations focus on hand contact and hand-object 2D bounding boxes, covering both egocentric and exocentric views.

⚠️ **Note: This is human activity data, not a robot dataset.** It is commonly used to train hand detection and manipulation understanding models.

EPIC-KITCHENS-100 is a large-scale egocentric kitchen operation dataset published by **University of Bristol** and **University of Toronto**.

It contains **100 hours**, **20 million frames**, and **90K actions** across **700 videos** of varying lengths. All videos are captured with a head-mounted camera in **45 different** kitchen environments, recording long-duration unscripted daily activities.

⚠️ **Note: This is human activity data, not a robot dataset.** Used by the VLA community (e.g., RoboMirror) for egocentric visual representation pretraining.

Assembly101 is a fine-grained assembly action dataset published by **University of Bristol**.

It records people assembling and disassembling **101** 'take-apart' toy cars, with synchronized **8 static** and **4 egocentric** camera views. Sequences are annotated with **100K+** coarse-grained action segments, **1M** fine-grained actions, and **18M** 3D hand poses.

⚠️ **Note: This is human activity data, not a robot dataset.** However, it is highly relevant to robot assembly tasks — 3D hand poses can inform dexterous manipulation policies.

notes: ⚠️ Human motion data, NOT a robot dataset. 15.6M full-body poses, 81.1K sequences with SMPL-X. Reference for humanoid robot whole-body control via RoboMirror-style retargeting.

Motion-X is a large-scale 3D full-body human motion dataset published by **Tsinghua University** and **Shanghai AI Lab**.

It contains **15.6M** full-body poses and **81.1K** motion sequences annotated with **SMPL-X** parameters (including hand poses), along with action labels, text labels, and RGB video.

⚠️ **Note: This is human motion data, not a robot dataset.** However, via RoboMirror-style retargeting, it can be transferred to humanoid robots for training whole-body locomotion and manipulation policies.

Ego-Exo4D is a large-scale multi-view skilled human activity dataset published by **Meta AI** and 13 universities.

It simultaneously captures **egocentric** and **exocentric** views of skilled human activities (sports, music, dance, bike repair, etc.). **740 participants** across **13 cities** performed activities in **123 natural scenes**, totaling **1,286 hours**.

⚠️ **Note: This is human activity data, not a robot dataset.** It is used by methods like RoboMirror for robot skill learning — the egocentric perspective is particularly suited for training visuomotor policies.

name: AMASS
institution: Max Planck Institute / CMU / ETH Zürich
scale: 40+ 小时动作捕捉，11,000+ 动作序列，720 万帧，300+ 对象
notes: ⚠️ Human motion data, NOT a robot dataset. THE standard humanoid motion benchmark. 40h mocap, 11K+ sequences, 7.2M frames. AstraBrain-WBC 0.5 training data is 2 orders of magnitude larger than AMASS.
description:
AMASS (Archive of Motion Capture as Surface Shapes) is a large-scale human motion dataset published by **Max Planck Institute**, **CMU**, and **ETH Zürich**.

It unifies **15** different motion capture data sources (CMU MoCap, HumanEva, KIT, BMLrub, etc.), covering **40+ hours** of motion data, **11,000+** motion sequences, and approximately **7.2 million frames** across **300+ subjects**. All data is represented using unified **SMPL/SMPL-X** body model parameters.

⚠️ **Note: This is human motion data, not a robot dataset.** It is the standard benchmark in humanoid robot motion control, widely used for training and evaluating whole-body motion tracking, retargeting, and imitation learning. The AstraBrain-WBC 0.5 paper uses AMASS as a comparison baseline (its training data is 2 orders of magnitude larger).

dataFormat.schema: sequence → poses (SMPL/SMPL-X) / shape / gender
dataFormat.layout: NPZ 格式，每序列含 SMPL/SMPL-H/SMPL-X 姿态参数 + 体型参数 + 性别标签

name: PHUMA
scale: 73 小时，76,000+ 片段，Unitree G1 & H1-2
notes: ⚠️ Human motion data (retargeted), NOT a robot dataset. Physically-grounded humanoid locomotion. 73h, 76K+ clips, Unitree G1 & H1-2. 3× AMASS scale. Used by AstraBrain-WBC 0.5 and NVIDIA ProtoMotions.
description:
PHUMA (Physically-grounded HUMAnoid locomotion dataset) is a large-scale physically-aware humanoid locomotion dataset published by **KAIST DAVIAN Robotics** lab.

It contains **73 hours** of motion data and **76,000+** motion clips, targeting **Unitree G1** and **H1-2** humanoid robots. PHUMA filters out ~70% of raw Humanoid-X data artifacts (jitter, floating, ground penetration, foot-skating, joint violations) via a physics-aware curation pipeline, and uses proprietary PhySINK retargeting to ensure physical plausibility.

⚠️ **Note: This is human motion retargeting data, not real robot data.** In imitation learning benchmarks, PHUMA achieves **1.2×** and **2.1×** higher success rates than AMASS and Humanoid-X respectively. Used by AstraBrain-WBC 0.5 and NVIDIA ProtoMotions.

dataFormat.index: 无

name: LAFAN1
notes: ⚠️ Human motion data, NOT a robot dataset. Ubisoft animation dataset. 77 sequences, 496K frames, 17 themes (dance, martial arts, falls, obstacles). AstraBrain-WBC 0.5 training source, retargeted to Unitree G1.
description:
LAFAN1 is a high-quality motion capture dataset published by **Ubisoft La Forge** for human motion prediction and generation research.

It contains **77** motion sequences, approximately **496K frames** (~4.6 hours), spanning **17 themes**: obstacles, walking, dance, fall & recovery, aiming, crawling, running, fight, jumps, push & stumble, sprint, etc. Recorded from **5** actors in a motion capture studio.

⚠️ **Note: This is human motion data, not a robot dataset.** In humanoid robotics, LAFAN1 is widely used for motion retargeting — both AstraBrain-WBC 0.5 and the Unitree LAFAN1 Retargeting Dataset use LAFAN1 as source data.

dataFormat.schema: file → frames → joint_hierarchy / rotations / root_positions
dataFormat.layout: BVH 文件（Biovision Hierarchy），每文件含骨骼层级 + 关节旋转 + 根位置
dataFormat.index: 无

name: Motion-X++
notes: ⚠️ Human motion data, NOT a robot dataset. Significantly expanded Motion-X: 120.5K clips, 19.5M frames, 180.9h, new RGB video + audio, GPT-4V annotations. AstraBrain-WBC 0.5 training source. Requires authorization.
description:
Motion-X++ is a significantly expanded version of the Motion-X dataset published by **Tsinghua University**, **IDEA**, **JHU**, and **CUHK-Shenzhen**.

Compared to the original Motion-X (15.6M frames), Motion-X++ contains **120.5K** motion clips, **19.5M** frames, **180.9 hours**, plus **80.8K RGB videos** and **45.3K** audio samples. GPT-4V is used for frame-level semantic annotation.

⚠️ **Note: This is human motion data, not a robot dataset.** Used by AstraBrain-WBC 0.5 as one of its training data sources for humanoid whole-body motion control. Requires authorization request to download from HuggingFace.
